_note: Captured from the cupertino documentation index on 2026-05-30 via the MCP search/list tools. Real framework names and document counts, real Apple titles and abstracts, across every source (database). Life-like mock content for exercising every search option until CupertinoDataEngine ships. An expandable subset, not the full corpus.

documents.title: SwiftUI
documents.summary: Declare the user interface and behavior for your app on every platform.
documents.markdown:
# SwiftUI

Declare the user interface and behavior for your app on every platform.

## Overview

SwiftUI provides views, controls, and layout structures for declaring your app's user interface. The framework provides event handlers for delivering taps, gestures, and other input, and tools to manage the flow of data from your app's models down to the views.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: View Management
documents.summary: Manage your user interface, including the size and position of views in a window.
documents.markdown:
# View Management

Manage your user interface, including the size and position of views in a window.

## Topics

- NSWindowController, NSViewController
- NSSplitViewController, NSSplitView, NSSplitViewItem

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: NSStackView
documents.summary: A view that arranges an array of views horizontally or vertically and updates their placement and sizing when the window size changes.
documents.markdown:
# NSStackView

A view that arranges an array of views horizontally or vertically and updates their placement and sizing when the window size changes.

```swift
class NSStackView
```

documents.title: UIKit
documents.summary: Construct and manage a graphical, event-driven user interface for your iOS, iPadOS, or tvOS app.
documents.markdown:
# UIKit

Construct and manage a graphical, event-driven user interface for your iOS, iPadOS, or tvOS app.

## Overview

UIKit provides the window and view architecture for implementing your UI, the event-handling infrastructure for delivering Multi-Touch and other input, and the main run loop for managing interactions between the user, the system, and your app.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: Foundation
documents.summary: Access essential data types, collections, and operating-system services to define the base layer of functionality for your app.
documents.markdown:
# Foundation

Access essential data types, collections, and operating-system services to define the base layer of functionality for your app.

## Topics

- Numbers, Data, and Basic Values
- Strings and Text
- Collections
- Dates and Times

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: Combine
documents.summary: Customize handling of asynchronous events by combining event-processing operators.
documents.markdown:
# Combine

Customize handling of asynchronous events by combining event-processing operators.

## Overview

The Combine framework provides a declarative Swift API for processing values over time. Combine declares publishers to expose values that can change over time, and subscribers to receive those values from the publishers.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: SwiftData
documents.summary: Write your model code declaratively to add managed persistence and efficient model fetching.
documents.markdown:
# SwiftData

Write your model code declaratively to add managed persistence and efficient model fetching.

## Overview

Combining Core Data's proven persistence technology and Swift's modern concurrency features, SwiftData enables you to add persistence to your app quickly, with minimal code and no external dependencies.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: Observable()
documents.summary: Defines and implements conformance of the Observable protocol.
documents.markdown:
# Observable()

Defines and implements conformance of the Observable protocol.

```swift
macro Observable()
```

This macro adds observation support to a custom type and conforms it to the `Observable` protocol.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: WidgetKit
documents.summary: Extend the reach of your app by creating widgets, watch complications, Live Activities, and controls.
documents.markdown:
# WidgetKit

Extend the reach of your app by creating widgets, watch complications, Live Activities, and controls.

## Overview

Using WidgetKit, you can make your app's content available in contexts outside the app. On iPhone and iPad, people put widgets in Today View, on the Home Screen, and on the Lock Screen.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: Sidebars
documents.summary: A sidebar appears on the leading side of a view and lets people navigate between sections in your app or game.
documents.markdown:
# Sidebars

A sidebar appears on the leading side of a view and lets people navigate between sections in your app or game. It floats above content, giving a broad, flat view of an app's information hierarchy.

**Best practice:** extend content beneath the sidebar so it reads as a floating layer.

_Captured from the cupertino index (hig)._

documents.title: Split views
documents.summary: A split view manages the presentation of multiple adjacent panes of content.
documents.markdown:
# Split views

A split view manages the presentation of multiple adjacent panes of content. Selecting an item in the primary pane displays its contents in the secondary pane.

**Best practice:** persistently highlight the current selection in each pane that leads to the detail view. Prefer a split view in a regular, not compact, environment.

_Captured from the cupertino index (hig)._

documents.title: Layout
documents.summary: A consistent layout that adapts to various contexts makes your experience more approachable.
documents.markdown:
# Layout

A consistent layout that adapts to various contexts makes your experience more approachable and helps people enjoy their apps on all their devices.

**Best practice:** group related items, and give essential information enough space.

_Captured from the cupertino index (hig)._

documents.title: Actors
documents.summary: Introduces actors to Swift to protect mutable state from data races.
documents.markdown:
# SE-0306: Actors

Status: Implemented (Swift 5.5).

Actors protect their mutable state from concurrent access. An async function may be actor-isolated, meaning it formally runs on the actor's executor.

_Captured from the cupertino index (swift-evolution)._

documents.title: Concurrency in Top-level Code
documents.summary: Bringing concurrency to top-level code, protecting top-level variables from data races.
documents.markdown:
# SE-0343: Concurrency in Top-level Code

Status: Implemented (Swift 5.7).

This proposal irons out how concurrency works in top-level code, focusing on how top-level variables are protected from data races and how a synchronous context becomes asynchronous.

_Captured from the cupertino index (swift-evolution)._

documents.title: Concurrency Interoperability with Objective-C
documents.summary: Interoperating Swift concurrency with Objective-C completion-handler methods.
documents.markdown:
# SE-0297: Concurrency Interoperability with Objective-C

Status: Implemented (Swift 5.5).

Defines how Swift's async functions and actors interoperate with Objective-C asynchronous completion-handler methods.

_Captured from the cupertino index (swift-evolution)._

documents.title: Advanced Operators
documents.summary: Define custom operators, perform bitwise operations, and use builder syntax.
documents.markdown:
# Advanced Operators

Define custom operators, perform bitwise operations, and use builder syntax. Unlike C, arithmetic operators in Swift do not overflow by default; overflow is trapped and reported as an error unless you opt in with the overflow operators (`&+`, `&-`, `&*`).

_Captured from the cupertino index (swift-book)._

documents.title: Patterns
documents.summary: Match and destructure values.
documents.markdown:
# Patterns

A pattern represents the structure of a single value or a composite value, so you can match a variety of values. There are two basic kinds: those that always match, and those that may fail to match at runtime.

_Captured from the cupertino index (swift-book)._

documents.title: Core Animation Basics
documents.summary: Core Animation provides a general purpose system for animating views and other visual elements of your app.
documents.markdown:
# Core Animation Basics

Core Animation provides a general purpose system for animating views and other visual elements. It integrates with views to provide better performance by caching their contents into bitmaps the graphics hardware can manipulate directly.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-archive)._

documents.title: Transition Animation
documents.summary: Transition animation is used when it is impossible to interpolate the effect of changing a layer property.
documents.markdown:
# Transition Animation

Transition animation is used when it is impossible to mathematically interpolate the effect of changing the value of a layer property. The `CATransition` class provides transition functionality, a direct subclass of `CAAnimation`.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-archive)._

documents.title: AsyncAlgorithms
documents.summary: An open-source package of asynchronous sequence and advanced algorithms that involve concurrency.
documents.markdown:
# AsyncAlgorithms

Swift Async Algorithms is an open-source package of asynchronous sequence and advanced algorithms that involve concurrency, along with their related types. Includes operators like `chunked`, `adjacentPairs`, and collection initializers from async sequences.

_Captured from the cupertino index (packages)._

documents.title: NSCollectionViewFlowLayout
documents.summary: A layout that organizes items into a flexible and configurable arrangement.
documents.markdown:
# NSCollectionViewFlowLayout

A layout that organizes items into a flexible and configurable arrangement. The first item is positioned in the top-left corner and others are laid out horizontally or vertically based on the scroll direction.

```swift
class NSCollectionViewFlowLayout
```

documents.title: ObservationIgnored()
documents.summary: Disables observation tracking of a property.
documents.markdown:
# ObservationIgnored()

Disables observation tracking of a property.

```swift
macro ObservationIgnored()
```

By default an object can observe any accessible property of an observable type; attach this macro to prevent observation of a property.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: Swift Charts
documents.summary: Construct and customize charts on every Apple platform.
documents.markdown:
# Swift Charts

Construct and customize charts on every Apple platform. Swift Charts is a flexible framework for making informative and delightful charts using a declarative syntax familiar from SwiftUI.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-docs)._

documents.title: Clarify the Execution of Non-Actor-Isolated Async Functions
documents.summary: Clarifies where asynchronous functions that are not actor-isolated run, and tightens sendability checking.
documents.markdown:
# SE-0338: Clarify the Execution of Non-Actor-Isolated Async Functions

Status: Implemented (Swift 5.7). Clarifies that non-actor-isolated async functions do not run on any actor's executor, and tightens sendability checking to avoid a potential data race.

_Captured from the cupertino index (swift-evolution)._

documents.title: Function Back Deployment
documents.summary: Introduces @backDeployed to ship new API implementations back to older runtimes.
documents.markdown:
# SE-0376: Function Back Deployment

Status: Implemented (Swift 5.8). The `@backDeployed` attribute lets a library ship the implementation of a new function so it runs on OS versions released before the API was introduced.

_Captured from the cupertino index (swift-evolution)._

documents.title: Animation Class Roadmap
documents.summary: Core Animation provides an expressive set of animation classes you can use in your application.
documents.markdown:
# Animation Class Roadmap

Core Animation provides an expressive set of animation classes. `CAAnimation` is the abstract base; `CAPropertyAnimation`, `CABasicAnimation`, and `CAKeyframeAnimation` build on it for property and keyframe animation.

_Captured from the cupertino index (apple-archive)._

documents.title: Chunked
documents.summary: Group elements of an async sequence into chunks by count, time, or projection.
documents.markdown:
# Chunked

From the swift-async-algorithms package. The `chunked` operators group elements of an `AsyncSequence` into chunks by count, by time signal, or by a projected grouping key.

_Captured from the cupertino index (packages)._

documents.title: Setting up Cursor for Swift Development
documents.summary: Use the Swift VS Code extension with Cursor for syntax highlighting, navigation, debugging, and testing.
documents.markdown:
# Setting up Cursor for Swift Development

Cursor is a fork of VS Code, so the Swift VS Code extension works with it: syntax highlighting and code completion, Go to Definition and Find All References, Swift Package Manager support, debugging, and testing with XCTest or Swift Testing.

_Captured from the cupertino index (swift-org)._
